prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Oh, my dear, a CPU is like the brain of your computer—it’s the little piece inside that does all the thinking! You know how your brain tells your body what to do, like move your fingers or solve a puzzle? Well, the CPU does the same thing for the computer, telling it how to run games, open pictures, or even help you do your homework. It’s very fast and works really hard, even though you can’t see it! Some computers have stronger CPUs, just like some people are better at solving tricky problems than others. It’s a very important part, but don’t worry—you don’t have to understand all the details, just like you don’t need to know how your heart works to love someone!
